channel.title: SevenAtSevenSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: U.S. and Iran to hold nuclear talks. And, Harvard professor resigns over Epstein ties
item.description: U.S. and Iranian officials are set to meet today in Geneva to discuss Tehran's nuclear program. And, Harvard professor Larry Summers is resigning over...

item.title: Tariffs cost American shoppers. They're unlikely to get that money back
item.description: After the Supreme Court declared the emergency tariffs illegal, the refund process will be messy and will go to businesses first.

item.title: Pentagon shifts toward maintaining ties to Scouting
item.description: Months after NPR reported on the Pentagon's efforts to sever ties with Scouting America, efforts to maintain the partnership have new momentum

item.title: Every business wants your review. What's with the feedback frenzy?
item.description: Customers want to read reviews and businesses need reviews to attract customers. But the constant demand for reviews could be creating a feedback back...

item.title: Why farmers in California are backing a giant solar farm
item.description: Many farmers have had to fallow land as a state law comes into effect limiting their access to water. There's now a push to develop some of that land ...

item.title: Memorial services for Jesse Jackson begin at Chicago headquarters of his civil rights organization
Instagram now alerts parents if their teen searches for suicide or self-harm content
item.description: Parents will be informed if their teen searches for suicide or self-harm content and offered resources.

item.title: Everything Samsung revealed at its Galaxy Unpacked event
item.description: Samsung's new privacy screen feature on Galaxy S26 Ultra was the most notable feature of the event.

item.title: You Have Until 2031: What Happens When Population Goes Negative?
item.description: There’s a ticking time bomb for the U.S. housing market that nobody is talking about. It’s the biggest existential threat to home prices and housing d...

item.title: Gushwork bets on AI search for customer leads — and early results are emerging
item.description: Gushwork has raised $9 million in a seed round led by SIG and Lightspeed. The startup has seen early customer traction from AI search tools like ChatG...

item.title: Most Americans see Iran as an enemy but doubt Trump’s judgment on military force, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: A new AP-NORC poll finds that as the U.S. and Iran head into their next round of nuclear talks in Geneva, many U.S. adults continue to view Iran’s nuc...

item.title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff: This isn’t our first SaaSpocalypse
item.description: Salesforce reported a solid year-end earnings and then pulled out all the stops to ward off more talk of the death of its business to AI.

item.title: How to avoid bad hires in early-stage startups
item.description: Lucena got the idea for Mappa after trying to build a marketing team but continually feeling like she had made the wrong hires.

item.title: 5 Things We’d Do If We Were Starting Over in Real Estate Today
item.description: Still stuck on step one in your investing journey? There are countless success stories from investors who started five, 10, or 20 years ago. But getti...

item.title: What to know about the LA superintendent whose home was searched by FBI
The Landlord Reset: 7 Financial Moves to Make Before Q2 Sneaks Up on You
item.description: This article is presented by Avail. Did you know that if you’re a landlord, February is life’s gift to you in terms of getting your business finances ...

item.title: 6 Numbers You Need to Know Before Buying a Rental Property
item.description: There are six numbers you need to know before buying a rental property. We run these numbers before we buy any investment, and knowing all six gives y...

item.title: A Return to Pre-2008 Lending Rules? Banks Are Ready to Open the Financial Floodgates to Borrowers
item.description: Okay, so liar loans and the opportunity to buy a home in a deceased relative’s name might not be coming back anytime soon. However, the wild-and-windy...
